title: The 11 Best Fitness Studio Management Software
subtitle: An in-depth ranking of the top platforms for managing classes, members, payments, and marketing for boutique fitness studios.
audience: Boutique fitness studio owners managing classes, members, and billing

editor.name: Top 11 Editorial
editor.credential: Autonomous AI ranking engine — methodology v1.0 weights public
editor.conflict_disclosure: None. The editor of Top 11 is not a candidate on this list.

independence.statement: Top 11 takes no payment from any provider on this list. Scores are computed from a public weighted rubric; methodology weights were locked before entry research began.

changelog.text: Initial publication. Methodology v1.0 weights Core Studio Management (30%), Billing & POS (25%), Client Engagement & Marketing (20%), Ease of Use & Support (15%), and Integrations & Ecosystem (10%).

answer_capsule: The best fitness studio management software is Mindbody for its comprehensive features, followed by Momence for its modern user experience and WellnessLiving for its all-in-one value.

criteria.name: Core Studio Management
criteria.description: Effectiveness of class scheduling, appointment booking, waitlists, membership management, staff payroll, and reporting.

criteria.name: Billing & POS
criteria.description: Reliability of payment processing, recurring billing, contract automation, and integrated point-of-sale for retail.

criteria.name: Client Engagement & Marketing
criteria.description: Quality of the branded mobile app, automated email/SMS campaigns, review management, and lead capture tools.

criteria.name: Ease of Use & Support
criteria.description: Intuitive design of the admin and client interfaces, quality of customer support, and smoothness of the onboarding process.

criteria.name: Integrations & Ecosystem
criteria.description: Availability of key integrations like website widgets, accounting software, video-on-demand platforms, and API access.

guide.q: What is fitness studio management software?
guide.a: It's an all-in-one platform that helps studio owners manage daily operations. This typically includes class scheduling, member check-in, automated billing and payments, staff management, retail sales (POS), and marketing tools to attract and retain clients.

guide.q: Why do I need specialized software instead of generic schedulers?
guide.a: Specialized software is built for the unique needs of a fitness business. It handles complex membership types (e.g., 10-class packs vs. unlimited monthly), automated recurring payments, liability waivers, waitlists, and integrates with fitness-specific tools like performance tracking or video-on-demand libraries, which generic tools lack.

how_to_choose: First, map your core needs: Are you class-based, appointment-based, or hybrid? Do you sell retail? This will narrow the field.
Second, evaluate the client experience. Test the booking process and mobile app from a member's perspective. A clunky interface can deter clients.
Third, request a full pricing quote, including payment processing fees and any setup or hardware costs. The monthly subscription is only part of the total cost.
Finally, check reviews for customer support quality. When your payment system goes down at 6 AM, you need responsive, effective help.

faqs.q: What's the difference between Mindbody and Vagaro?
faqs.a: Mindbody is a fitness-first platform with deep, complex features tailored for large studios and wellness centers, including a powerful consumer marketplace. Vagaro serves a broader market, including salons and spas, and is often praised for its user-friendly interface, built-in marketing tools, and more straightforward pricing.

faqs.q: How much does fitness studio software typically cost?
faqs.a: Prices range widely, from around $100/month for basic plans for small studios to over $500/month for premium or enterprise plans with advanced features, multi-location support, and branded mobile apps. Always ask about payment processing fees, which are an additional cost.

faqs.q: Do I need a platform with a branded mobile app?
faqs.a: A branded app provides a premium, seamless experience for your members, boosting retention and making booking easier. While not essential for brand new studios, it's a key feature for established businesses looking to strengthen their brand and simplify the client journey.

faqs.q: Can this software handle both in-person and online virtual classes?
faqs.a: Yes, most modern platforms are designed for hybrid fitness models. They integrate with streaming services like Zoom or have built-in video-on-demand (VOD) hosting, allowing you to manage and monetize both your physical and digital offerings from one place.

honest_disclosures: Most top-tier providers are US-based, and support or feature parity for international customers can sometimes be a secondary priority.
Stated monthly prices often exclude mandatory payment processing fees, which can significantly impact the total cost of ownership.
Data migration from an existing system can be complex and may incur additional one-time fees.

glossary.definition: A mobile application for iOS and Android that is customized with a fitness studio's own logo, colors, and branding, allowing members to book classes and manage their account directly.

entries.name: Mindbody
entries.best_for: Established, multi-faceted fitness and wellness businesses that need the most comprehensive, enterprise-grade feature set on the market.
entries.verdict: Mindbody ranks first due to its unmatched feature depth, extensive integration ecosystem, and powerful consumer marketplace that actively drives new client acquisition.
entries.verdict_short: The most powerful and feature-complete platform for established fitness businesses.
entries.praise: Its reporting and analytics suite is best-in-class, providing deep insights into business performance and member behavior.
entries.criticism: The platform's complexity can lead to a steep learning curve, and its premium pricing and long-term contracts can be prohibitive for smaller studios.
entries.criticism_short: Complex, expensive, and has rigid contracts.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-10.

entries.name: Momence
entries.best_for: Modern studios, especially in yoga and pilates, that prioritize a seamless user experience for both staff and clients and require strong hybrid/VOD capabilities.
entries.verdict: Momence earns the second spot for its beautifully designed, intuitive interface and best-in-class tools for managing hybrid (in-person and virtual) fitness businesses.
entries.verdict_short: The best user experience with powerful tools for hybrid studios.
entries.praise: Its integrated video-on-demand and livestreaming tools are exceptionally well-executed, making it easy to manage and monetize digital content.
entries.criticism: As a newer player, its integration marketplace and advanced reporting features are still maturing compared to legacy providers like Mindbody.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-10.

entries.name: WellnessLiving
entries.best_for: Studios seeking a feature-rich, all-in-one alternative to Mindbody that offers better value and more responsive customer support.
entries.verdict: WellnessLiving secures third place by offering a comprehensive, all-in-one feature set comparable to top competitors but at a more accessible price point and with highly-rated customer service.
entries.verdict_short: A powerful, value-driven alternative to the market leaders.
entries.praise: The platform includes an impressive amount of built-in functionality, such as rewards programs and email marketing, reducing the need for third-party tools.
entries.criticism: While powerful, the user interface can feel dated and less intuitive in some areas compared to more modern platforms like Momence.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-10.

entries.name: Vagaro
entries.best_for: Appointment and class-based businesses that want powerful booking and marketing tools with simple, transparent, per-employee pricing.
entries.verdict: Vagaro stands out for its strong client booking experience, integrated marketing features, and a consumer marketplace, all delivered through a predictable, per-service-provider pricing model.
entries.verdict_short: Excellent booking and marketing with transparent, scalable pricing.
entries.praise: Its built-in tools for creating websites, running daily deals, and sending automated marketing campaigns are robust and user-friendly.
entries.criticism: While excellent for many studios, its feature set is less specialized for the nuanced needs of certain fitness niches like CrossFit compared to dedicated platforms.
entries.criticism_short: Less specialized for some fitness niches.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-10.

entries.name: Glofox (by ABC Fitness)
entries.best_for: Boutique fitness franchises and multi-location studios that require a premium, highly customizable branded app and robust global management tools.
entries.verdict: Glofox excels by providing a superior, deeply integrated branded mobile app experience and the administrative tools necessary for scaling and managing franchises or multiple studio locations.
entries.verdict_short: The top choice for franchises and studios focused on brand experience.
entries.praise: The quality, customization, and user experience of their member-facing mobile app are consistently rated as among the best in the industry.
entries.criticism: Pricing is not transparent and is generally positioned at the higher end of the market, making it less suitable for single, independent studios.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-10.

entries.name: Mariana Tek
entries.best_for: High-end, experience-focused boutique studios that demand a premium, design-forward platform for both their clients and staff.
entries.verdict: Mariana Tek earns its spot by delivering a sophisticated, aesthetically pleasing platform with a focus on user experience, making it a favorite among premium and celebrity-endorsed fitness brands.
entries.verdict_short: A sophisticated, design-forward platform for high-end brands.
entries.praise: The platform's user interface is exceptionally clean and modern, providing a luxury feel that aligns with high-end studio branding.
entries.criticism: It is one of the most expensive options available, and its feature set is tightly focused on class-based studios, lacking some of the broader functionality of competitors.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-10.

entries.name: PushPress
entries.best_for: CrossFit boxes and functional fitness gyms that need specialized features like workout tracking (WODs) and a community-centric approach.
entries.verdict: PushPress is the top choice for affiliate gyms because it was built by gym owners specifically for their needs, with excellent workout tracking and member management tools tailored to the CrossFit methodology.
entries.verdict_short: Purpose-built software for CrossFit boxes with great WOD tracking.
entries.praise: The 'Track' feature for programming and tracking daily workouts is seamlessly integrated and far superior to the generic class descriptions in other software.
entries.criticism: Its feature set is highly specialized, making it a less ideal fit for studios outside the functional fitness or CrossFit space, such as yoga or pilates.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-10.

entries.name: Zen Planner
entries.best_for: Martial arts schools, affiliate gyms, and fitness studios that need solid, time-tested software for member management and automated billing.
entries.verdict: Zen Planner is a reliable and long-standing solution with robust features for attendance tracking, skills progression, and automated billing, making it a strong fit for martial arts and CrossFit-style gyms.
entries.verdict_short: A reliable, long-standing choice with strong member management.
entries.praise: Its capabilities for tracking member progression, belt ranks, and skills are particularly well-suited for martial arts dojos and similar disciplines.
entries.criticism: The user interface for both admins and members feels less modern and intuitive than newer competitors on the market.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-10.

entries.name: TeamUp
entries.best_for: Independent instructors and smaller studios who value simplicity, flexibility, and outstanding customer support over an exhaustive feature list.
entries.verdict: TeamUp earns its rank by focusing on doing the essentials exceptionally well, offering a clean, easy-to-use platform backed by what is widely considered the best customer support in the industry.
entries.verdict_short: Simple, flexible software with best-in-class customer support.
entries.praise: Customer support is consistently and overwhelmingly praised as being fast, knowledgeable, and genuinely helpful.
entries.criticism: The platform lacks some of the advanced marketing automation tools and a branded mobile app offered by higher-ranked, more expensive competitors.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-10.

entries.name: Pike13
entries.best_for: Appointment-based businesses like personal training and swim schools, as well as class-based studios that prioritize mobile-first client management.
entries.verdict: Pike13 provides a solid, mobile-friendly platform with strong features for managing appointments, staff scheduling, and client relationships, making it a dependable choice for service-based fitness businesses.
entries.verdict_short: A solid, mobile-friendly choice for appointment-based fitness.
entries.praise: Its staff management and payroll reporting features are robust, simplifying the process of managing a diverse team of instructors and trainers.
entries.criticism: The client-facing interface and overall design are not as modern or polished as the top-ranked competitors, and it lacks a native branded app.
entries.criticism_short: Dated client interface, no branded app.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-10.

entries.name: Walla
entries.best_for: Forward-thinking studio owners frustrated with legacy software who are willing to adopt a newer platform built around modern workflows and conversion optimization.
entries.verdict: Walla is our wildcard pick because it's a fast-emerging disruptor, rethinking studio management from the ground up with a focus on smart, automated workflows and a user experience that actively helps convert leads and retain members.
entries.verdict_short: An emerging disruptor focused on smart, conversion-driven workflows.
entries.praise: Its built-in conversion tools, like the 'Buy Now' feature that prompts waitlisted clients to purchase a plan, are innovative and directly impact revenue.
entries.criticism: As the newest platform on this list, its feature set is not as comprehensive, and its integration ecosystem is significantly smaller than its more established competitors.

risk_signals.summary: As a young, venture-backed company, it carries more market risk than established players.
